subject: math

production_standard.manifest_hub_exposure: All packages must appear through manifest/graph-driven discovery, not hardcoded placeholders.

supported_or_extendable_from_grades_1_3.notes: Reusable for Grade 4 whole-number rounding, fraction number lines, and measurement contexts; must support larger labels, fractional ticks, and clearer benchmark labels.
Reusable for whole-number and fraction comparisons; Grade 4 packages should also introduce `comparison_model` when multiplicative comparison requires ratio language or times-as-many visuals.
Existing Grades 1-3 chart concepts are reusable, but Grade 4 needs named places through millions, commas, period grouping, and explicit zero placeholders.
Reusable for base-ten decomposition; Grade 4 needs standard, word, expanded, and expanded-notation variants through 1,000,000.
Reusable midpoint model; Grade 4 needs nearest ten, hundred, thousand, ten-thousand, and hundred-thousand intervals.
Reusable for multiplication and area/perimeter contexts; Grade 4 multiplication needs multi-digit partitions, labels, and partial products.
Reusable from Grade 3 multiplication foundations; Grade 4 needs up-to-four-digits-by-one-digit and two-digits-by-two-digits metadata.
Reusable from Grade 3 division foundations; Grade 4 needs long-division steps, quotient place value, and remainder display.
Reusable for checking division with multiplication and inverse-operation prompts.
Reusable for equivalent fractions and fraction operations; Grade 4 needs unlike-denominator comparison and whole-number multiples of unit fractions.
Reusable for fraction equivalence and like-denominator operations where circle partitions are appropriate.
Reusable for whole-number multiplication; Grade 4 needs fraction repeated-addition narration and notation.
Reusable for fraction operation setup and word-problem equations; needs mixed-number and unit support.
Reusable for multi-step measurement, fraction, and operation contexts; Grade 4 needs units, equations, and answer labels.
Reusable for data displays; Grade 4 needs fractional measurement labels and fraction-answer normalization.
Reusable for area/perimeter word problems and geometry contexts.
Reusable for shape classification; Grade 4 needs lines, angles, symmetry, parallel/perpendicular attributes, and hierarchy metadata.

new_renderers_needed.need: Multiplicative comparison visual with groups, times-as-many labels, and additive-vs-multiplicative contrast.
Factor-pair tiles/table showing products, missing factors, and factor vs multiple language.
Highlighted skip-count/multiple chart for factors, multiples, prime/composite reasoning, and common multiples.
Input/output and shape/number pattern table with rule callouts and missing-term slots.
Column-aligned standard algorithm renderer for addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division step explanations.
Explicit trade/regrouping visual for multi-digit subtraction, including regrouping across zeros.
Number line for estimate-first and reasonableness checks with rounded addends/subtrahends/products.
Multi-digit multiplication partial-products grid/table connected to area model and standard algorithm.
Division visual that shows quotient groups, leftovers, and context-specific remainder interpretation.
Conversion table for larger-to-smaller units with multiplicative scale factors and unit labels.
Angle visual with vertex, rays, degree measure labels, and acute/right/obtuse categories.
Interactive/static protractor view with baseline, vertex placement, inner/outer scale reading, and target angle.
Parallel, perpendicular, intersecting, ray, line, and segment relationship visual.
Shape symmetry visual with candidate fold lines and reflection matching.

schema_updates_needed: Require generator mappings for Story, Lesson, Watch, Demo, Practice, Challenge, Checkpoint, Badge, and Profile.
Require Grade 4 Math level_banks with four focused levels plus Mixed and 10-12 questions per level.
Enumerate or runtime-enforce all Grade 4 Math visual_model and question_type values.
Add metadata for Grade 4 operands, algorithm steps, fraction forms, measurement units, geometry attributes, acceptable equivalent forms, and voice narration.
Require manifest/graph/hub metadata and readiness checkpoint metadata.

backlog.title: Finalize Grade 4 Math SkillPackage schema/runtime contract
acceptance_criteria: Schema or registry mappings cover every Grade 4 visual_model and question_type in this plan.
Validation rejects packages without Story through Profile guided mission source data or deterministic generator mappings.
Validation rejects Practice Center level_banks that do not include four focused levels plus Mixed with 10-12 questions each.
Grade 4 package metadata includes domain, focus, standards tags, misconception tags, voice metadata, and manifest exposure metadata.

backlog.title: Build or extend Grade 4 Math visual renderers
acceptance_criteria: Renderer registry covers supported/extended and new Grade 4 visual models without fallback placeholder output.
Each renderer has a canonical fixture and test asserting data-renderer output.
Math-specific renderers expose aria labels and speakable descriptions for voice narration.

backlog.title: Guarantee Skill Practice Center generation from level_banks
acceptance_criteria: Every Grade 4 Math package exposes Level 1, Level 2, Level 3, Level 4, and Mixed from package data.
Each level renders 10-12 production questions, mastery state, feedback, and Read Question controls.
No Skill Practice Center cards or questions are hardcoded outside package/manifest data.

backlog.title: Wire Grade 4 Math packages through manifest/graph/hub discovery
acceptance_criteria: Grade 4 Math appears only after packages are listed in the content manifest/graph.
Hub cards use SkillPackage metadata, not hardcoded placeholders.
Readiness checkpoints prevent incomplete packages from appearing as production-ready.

backlog.title: Implement full Grade 4 Math voice standard
acceptance_criteria: Every guided mission screen includes Read This Page text and label.
Practice, Challenge, and Checkpoint questions include Read Question text.
All Practice Center questions include Read Question text.
Narration routes through /api/skill-world/audio when cached AI audio is available and falls back to browser speech when not available.
Math symbols, fractions, comparisons, exponents of place value, and units are spoken naturally.

backlog.title: Add Grade 4 acceptable-answer normalization
acceptance_criteria: Numeric answers normalize commas, words, equations, and standard notation where appropriate.
Fraction answers normalize equivalent fractions, mixed numbers, improper fractions, and spacing.
Comparison answers accept symbols and words.
Measurement answers validate required units and conversion equivalence.
Algorithm responses can validate final answer plus step fields when the item requires them.
